title: Centotheca malabarica
taxonomy: Centotheca malabarica is a species of flowering plant in the family Poaceae. It belongs to the subfamily Panicoideae. This grass-like plant is native to South Asia, specifically in India and Sri Lanka. The classification of Centotheca malabarica places it in the genus Centotheca. Further classification places it in the family Poaceae, which includes over 12,000 species of grasses.
uses: Centotheca malabarica is known to have various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The plant has been traditionally used in some cultures to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues. Its leaves and stems are also believed to have wound-healing properties due to their rich content of antioxidants and other bioactive compounds. Furthermore, studies have suggested that Centotheca malabarica may possess antidiabetic properties, although more research is needed to confirm these findings. Additional research is also required to fully understand the plant's potential medicinal uses and benefits.

intro: Centotheca malabarica is a tropical plant species, commonly known as the "Indian feather grass," belonging to the family Poaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include its potential to reduce inflammation, exhibit antimicrobial activity, and modulate the immune system. The bioactive constituents of Centotheca malabarica include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. Traditional herbal preparations made from Centotheca malabarica include decoctions and infusions, which are used to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
